content:
I tested the point cloud in ISTRAM, I found out that because the point cloud is identical to the as-built terrain, by using the pointcloud instead of the original terrain we loose the cuts that should be present in the project as the software relies on the pointcloud as terrain. So the model must be built using the original terrain in order to retain the slopes that are supposed to go into the project.

Because of this, then we will need to rely on civil3D do to the north south extension of the slopes as we did last time. But, the overall topography of the place should be improved with the use of the TTP that is generated from ISTRAM together with the pointcloud and the ortophotography. So civil3D should be used to work on this surfaces to improve them and then coordinate them with the resulting ISTRAM/REVIT model to effectively represent the as-built terrain. Log this activity on our tasks file and let the update of the E377 as built model as pending work for next week.

Also include in the done tasks of today if you haven't already the meeting we had with Cristopher Galvez from istram. Also include a short meeting we had with Emmanuel Corrales from the AIF to clarify in regards of files.

Let me know when you are done as we will close the week now.
